notes:
PACK STRUCTURE (WOTC early era, pre-2002): 11 cards/pack = 7 common + 3 uncommon + 1 rare-or-better. Confirmed via Flipside Gaming's "Comprehensive Review of Rarity in the Pokemon TCG" which states the earliest WOTC sets had "four possible rarities: Commons, Uncommon, Rare (guaranteed 1 per pack), and Holo (approximately 1:3 packs)" and that the odds were printed on the packs. NO reverse-holo slot (reverse holos were introduced in Legendary Collection 2002 / e-Card series — Base Set predates them).

HOLO RATE: ~1 in 3 packs (Flipside Gaming + the canonical printed-on-pack WOTC odds, corroborated across multiple era sources). Mapped onto this set's buckets at the single rare-or-better slot: rare-holo = 1/3 = 0.3333, and the other 2/3 of packs the rare slot yields a non-holo rare -> rare = 0.6667. Slot sums to 1.0. Per-box expectation: 0.3333*36 = 12 holo rares/box, consistent with the well-known community figure of ~11-12 holos per Base Set 36-pack box.

BUCKET CHECK: distribution keys {rare-holo, rare, uncommon, common} are all within this set's available buckets {rare-holo:16, rare:16, uncommon:32, common:38}. Bulbapedia confirms the set has exactly 16 Rare Holo Pokemon (matching the rare-holo:16 bucket count) and 102 cards total. No ultra-rare/secret-rare bucket exists in Base Set (Crystal/secret rares are e-Card era, not WOTC Base).

PRODUCTS: 36-pack booster box confirmed via ToyWiz product listing ("Booster Box [36 Packs]") and DA Card World Base Set 1 booster box listing -> displayPacks=36. NO ETB existed in the WOTC era -> etbPacks=null. 11th card note: some pack breakdowns describe 5 common + 2 basic Energy rather than 7 common; Energy is not one of this set's rarity buckets, so following the era baseline the two non-uncommon/rare energy/common slots collapse into the 7-common filler (commons bucket). This does not affect the hit (rare-holo) economy.

VINTAGE CAVEAT: Base Set 36-pack boxes carry an enormous sealed collector premium (thousands of USD), so computed EV/booster from card values will sit far below box price — this is expected for vintage and rates are NOT inflated to compensate.

CONFIDENCE: low — the 1:3 holo rate is the era-standard printed-pack figure applied to this set without a dedicated large per-set opening study (vintage packs are too valuable to open at scale), corroborated by the Flipside Gaming era review and set composition (16 holos) but not a published statistical sample.
